Resolves #14606

Related: epic #14560 (parent) · #14592 (the density evidence — every bound here cites a measured figure) · #14831/PR #14827 (the merged component slice this re-freezes) · #14863/#14869 (the merged live-binding arc) · #14607 (the burst e2e — the cross-referenced sibling this deliberately does NOT absorb)

**The ticket's last open substance: the density re-freeze.** The 2026-07-05 scope-correction comment on the ticket split the component slice out (merged via PR #14827) and left #14606 open on the host-gated ACs. Those have since landed in the merged arc — this PR closes the loop with the measured bounds and maps every remaining AC to its witness:

- **AC-2 (live binding to the activity adapters)** — DELIVERED, merged arc: `FleetCockpit.loadActivity()` polls the read-observe `fleetActivity` bridge verb and routes its capability state honestly (`wired` → live even-when-quiet, `degraded` → the stale banner, absent/thrown → the labelled sample; fail-closed, never a blank).
- **AC-4 mount half (NL-verifiable live mount)** — WITNESSED: `test/playwright/e2e/agentos/Cockpit.spec.mjs:28` asserts the mounted `.fm-activity-stream` on the default Fleet keeper-view.
- **AC-5 (burst e2e)** — stays #14607's scope, as the ticket itself demands (cross-referenced, not duplicated).
- **The density bounds** — THIS PR, three of them, each frozen from a measured figure (sustained 0.5–1 events/min on a sprint day; ~3.6/min p95 burst):
  1. **The ring**: new `bufferSize` (200 ≈ 55min of the worst recorded burst) caps the HELD events in `beforeSetEvents` — drop-oldest, with the drop COUNTED into the fold. The held payload was unbounded before; now memory is bounded by construction and a drop is never silent.
  2. **Coalescing**: new pure `coalesceActivity(events, gapMs)` groups consecutive same-actor runs above ~2/min (gap < 30s) into ONE `×N` grouped row carrying the run's newest event — one busy actor can no longer flood every other off the glass. Honesty bounds the grouping: missing/out-of-order timestamps and anonymous events can't PROVE run membership, so each stands alone.
  3. **The window**: `maxVisible` 15 → 12 rows (the evidence froze 10–12), and the fold becomes the honest **"N earlier events"** line — counting EVENTS (folded rows' runs + ring drops), never rows.

Evidence: L2 (unit construct-probes) — `npx playwright test --config=<canonical unit config minus webServer> unit/apps/agentos/view/fleet/activityStream.spec.mjs unit/apps/agentos/view/fleet/fleetCockpit.spec.mjs unit/apps/agentos/view/fleet/fleetCockpitProjection.spec.mjs --workers=1` → **43/43 passed** at head `f0f12ba4c` (10 stream — 3 new density tests — plus both cockpit consumer suites regression-clean).

## Deltas from ticket

- The ticket's original numbers (visible ≈ 15, "virtualization + fold") predate the #14592 evidence; the epic-recorded density consequences (12-row window / 200-event ring / >2/min coalescing) supersede them. No virtualization below the measured scale — the ring + window keep the DOM ≤ header + 12 rows + fold at ANY event volume, which is the requirement virtualization was reaching for.
- The coalescing threshold and ring size are reactive configs (`coalesceGapMs`, `bufferSize`), so #14607's burst e2e can drive the bounds without touching the component.

## Test Evidence

- `activityStream.spec.mjs` 10/10 green — new: the pure coalescing rule matrix (run groups with count+newest; threshold-exact splits; distinct actors never group; unprovable membership stands alone; non-array degrades), the grouped `×5` render beside an un-grouped single, and the 500-event ring test (held events capped at 200, 12 default rows, fold `488 earlier events` = 188 folded + 300 dropped).
- `fleetCockpit.spec.mjs` + `fleetCockpitProjection.spec.mjs` regression-clean (the owner-held `streamEvents` re-materialization path unchanged).
- Pre-commit gates green (whitespace, shorthand, jsdoc-types, archaeology, block-alignment, aiconfig-test-mutation).

## Post-Merge Validation

- [ ] #14606 closes on merge; the epic ticks 19→20/35 (with the badge PR).
- [ ] #14607 (burst e2e) drives ≥3.6/min against THESE bounds — next leaf on the render spine.
